# Cloned Dočekal: What Did the Experiment Bring?
> 

Daniel Dočekal underwent an [experiment with a digital clone of his voice](https://365tipu.cz/2024/07/17/tip2749-konec-experimentu-s-hlasovym-digitalnim-ja-prakticke-postrehy-a-proc-prozatim-skoncil/) for podcasts. The audio quality is excellent and saves time on post-production. However, Dan points out that it requires investing extra time in writing scripts and corrections.

> “The quality of the resulting audio is incomparable to direct podcast recording.”\
> **— [Daniel Dočekal](https://365tipu.cz/2024/07/17/tip2749-konec-experimentu-s-hlasovym-digitalnim-ja-prakticke-postrehy-a-proc-prozatim-skoncil/), @365 tipů**

The technology proved effective, but according to Dočekal, it has limitations, such as an inability to deliver dramatic performance and problematic pronunciation of certain words. The price for the service used in the experiment, [Elevenlabs](http://elevenlabs.io/?from=partnerfrench7232)[1](#footnote-1), is acceptable, but the necessary editing work can be demanding.

Using a digital voice seems like an interesting solution, but Dan has not yet decided whether to continue with the clone.

We dealt with cloned voices in [Better Button #18](https://www.reknisioweb.cz/p/18#%C2%A7zivot-s-technologiemi).

<<<URL_0_7d4897>>

\#TYDEN ~ Weekly Overview 

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

What is the future of podcasts with AI voice cloning?

You are listening to \#tyden. Thursday's podcast, so don't be fooled into thinking it's Friday. A special episode, listenable on Substack and usual podcast platforms. Nothing costs anything, but the Sunday big \#tyden has a dense paid version. You'll please us if you become a subscriber. But now, let's get to the spoken word…
